24.reason: Missing address-of operator (&) before each variable; should be &l[i].x1, etc. This causes undefined behavior and likely a runtime error.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  Missing address-of operator (&) before each variable; should be &l[i].x1, etc. This causes undefined behavior and likely a runtime error.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: Missing address-of operator '&' before l[i].x1, l[i].y1, l[i].x2, l[i].y2. scanf requires pointers to store input values.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  Missing address-of operator & for each variable; should be &l[i].x1, &l[i].y1, &l[i].x2, &l[i].y2
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  Missing address-of operator '&' before each variable; causes undefined behavior (likely runtime error).
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The scanf function requires pointers to the variables where input should be stored. Passing l[i].x1 (an int) instead of &l[i].x1 (address of int) causes undefined behavior, likely a segmentation fault (Runtime Error).
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  [scanf requires pointers to variables (e.g., &l[i].x1), passing integers directly causes undefined behavior and likely a crash]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Missing '&' operator before l[i].x1 etc. in scanf leads to undefined behavior (writing to invalid memory), causing runtime error]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Missing '&' operator before l[i].x1 etc. in scanf leads to undefined behavior (writing to wrong memory locations), causing runtime error]
